proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with higher ordinal `feature_027` values have lower `objective_response` rates, with level 2 lower than level 0.

analyses.result_summary: `objective_response` declined monotonically across `feature_027`: level 0 rate 0.23096, level 1 rate 0.17428, and level 2 rate 0.12908. The one-level risk-difference slope was -0.05220.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_015` equal to 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_015` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_015` positive patients responded at 0.22098 versus 0.16507 when absent; risk difference +0.05591.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_025` equal to 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_025` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_025` positive patients responded at 0.23465 versus 0.16727 when absent; risk difference +0.06738.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_023` equal to 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_023` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_023` positive patients responded at 0.21958 versus 0.16608 when absent; risk difference +0.05350.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with higher continuous `feature_004` values have lower `objective_response` rates.

analyses.result_summary: A one-SD increase in `feature_004` was associated with a -0.02014 absolute response-rate difference. Top-quartile response was 0.15720 versus 0.20462 in the bottom quartile.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with higher continuous `feature_031` values have lower `objective_response` rates.

analyses.result_summary: A one-SD increase in `feature_031` was associated with a -0.01432 absolute response-rate difference. Top-quartile response was 0.16638 versus 0.20651 in the bottom quartile.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with higher continuous `feature_059` values have higher `objective_response` rates.

analyses.result_summary: A one-SD increase in `feature_059` was associated with a +0.00896 absolute response-rate difference. Top-quartile response was 0.19781 versus 0.17531 in the bottom quartile.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_001` equal to 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_001` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_001` positive patients responded at 0.16805 versus 0.19091 when absent; risk difference -0.02286.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_032` equal to 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_032` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_032` positive patients responded at 0.16570 versus 0.18991 when absent; risk difference -0.02422.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with higher continuous `feature_033` values have lower `objective_response` rates.
Patients with higher continuous `feature_053` values have lower `objective_response` rates.

analyses.result_summary: A one-SD increase in `feature_033` was associated with a -0.00790 absolute response-rate difference.
A one-SD increase in `feature_053` was associated with a -0.00653 absolute response-rate difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The main-effect associations for `feature_027`, `feature_015`, `feature_025`, `feature_023`, `feature_004`, `feature_031`, `feature_059`, `feature_001`, `feature_032`, `feature_033`, and `feature_053` persist after mutual adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: In a mutually adjusted linear probability model, all selected main effects retained their univariate directions. Adjusted effects included `feature_027` -0.03541 per SD, `feature_015` +0.05618, `feature_025` +0.06751, `feature_023` +0.05422, `feature_004` -0.02005 per SD, `feature_031` -0.01469 per SD, `feature_059` +0.00905 per SD, `feature_001` -0.02383, `feature_032` -0.02627, `feature_033` -0.00778 per SD, and `feature_053` -0.00662 per SD.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with both `feature_015` equal to 1 and `feature_025` equal to 1 have higher `objective_response` than expected from adding the separate effects of `feature_015` and `feature_025`.

analyses.result_summary: The `feature_015:feature_025` interaction coefficient was +0.19257. Joint-positive response was 0.34948 versus 0.16631 for `feature_015` only, 0.15851 for `feature_025` only, and 0.16791 for neither.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with both `feature_015` equal to 1 and `feature_023` equal to 1 have higher `objective_response` than expected from adding the separate effects of `feature_015` and `feature_023`.

analyses.result_summary: The `feature_015:feature_023` interaction coefficient was +0.15278. Joint-positive response was 0.30812 versus 0.16300 for `feature_015` only, 0.16049 for `feature_023` only, and 0.16814 for neither.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with both `feature_025` equal to 1 and `feature_023` equal to 1 have higher `objective_response` than expected from adding the separate effects of `feature_025` and `feature_023`.

analyses.result_summary: The `feature_025:feature_023` interaction coefficient was +0.19083. Joint-positive response was 0.34656 versus 0.15964 for `feature_025` only, 0.16492 for `feature_023` only, and 0.16883 for neither.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The high response associated with `feature_015`, `feature_025`, and `feature_023` is concentrated in patients where all three features equal 1.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates by count of positive features among `feature_015`, `feature_025`, and `feature_023` were 0.17179 for 0 features, 0.16242 for 1 feature, 0.16294 for 2 features, and 0.63307 for all 3 features. All-three-positive versus all others differed by about +0.4681.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After modeling the three-way interaction `feature_015:feature_025:feature_023`, the pairwise interactions among these three features are no longer the primary explanation of `objective_response`; the three-way interaction is positive.

analyses.result_summary: The three-way interaction coefficient was +0.46241. Pairwise coefficients shrank to +0.00833 (p=0.377) for `feature_015:feature_025`, +0.01391 (p=0.091) for `feature_015:feature_023`, and +0.00706 (p=0.451) for `feature_025:feature_023`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_001` equal to 1 attenuates the positive `objective_response` association of `feature_025` equal to 1.
`feature_001` equal to 1 attenuates the positive `objective_response` association of `feature_015` equal to 1.
`feature_001` equal to 1 attenuates the positive `objective_response` association of `feature_023` equal to 1.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_001:feature_025` had interaction coefficient -0.09870. Subgroup rates were 0.24825 for `feature_025` only versus 0.15838 when both `feature_001` and `feature_025` were present.
`feature_001:feature_015` had interaction coefficient -0.06307, indicating attenuation of the `feature_015` association among `feature_001` positive patients.
`feature_001:feature_023` had interaction coefficient -0.05194, indicating attenuation of the `feature_023` association among `feature_001` positive patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_032` equal to 1 attenuates the positive `objective_response` association of `feature_025` equal to 1.
`feature_032` equal to 1 attenuates the positive `objective_response` association of `feature_023` equal to 1.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_032:feature_025` had interaction coefficient -0.08772. Subgroup rates were 0.24237 for `feature_025` only versus 0.16534 when both `feature_032` and `feature_025` were present.
`feature_032:feature_023` had interaction coefficient -0.07276. Subgroup rates were 0.22596 for `feature_023` only versus 0.16357 when both `feature_032` and `feature_023` were present.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous adverse association of higher `feature_004` with lower `objective_response` persists in an integrated model containing the strongest binary interaction structure.

analyses.result_summary: In the integrated interaction model, the one-SD `feature_004` coefficient remained -0.02035.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_047` has a positive association with `objective_response`.
`feature_006` has a positive association with `objective_response`.
`feature_005` equal to 1 has a positive association with `objective_response`.
`feature_044` has a nonzero association with `objective_response`.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_047` had a +0.00341 one-SD association, but it did not meet p<0.05.
`feature_006` had a +0.00340 one-SD association, but it did not meet p<0.05.
`feature_005` positive patients had response 0.20234 versus 0.18675 when absent; risk difference +0.01558, not significant.
`feature_044` was essentially null: one-SD effect -0.00026.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with both `feature_040` equal to 1 and `feature_046` equal to 1 have higher `objective_response` than expected from adding the separate effects of `feature_040` and `feature_046`.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_040:feature_046` showed a smaller positive interaction: coefficient +0.04785, joint-positive response 0.21168 versus 0.18713 for `feature_040` only, 0.16476 for `feature_046` only, and 0.18807 for neither.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with both `feature_071` equal to 1 and `feature_050` equal to 1 have lower `objective_response` than expected from adding the separate effects of `feature_071` and `feature_050`.
Patients with both `feature_034` equal to 1 and `feature_074` equal to 1 have lower `objective_response` than expected from adding the separate effects of `feature_034` and `feature_074`.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_071:feature_050` had interaction coefficient -0.04331; joint-positive response 0.15722 versus 0.18982 for `feature_071` only, 0.19690 for `feature_050` only, and 0.18619 for neither.
`feature_034:feature_074` had interaction coefficient -0.03699; joint-positive response 0.15031 versus rates near 0.187-0.189 in the other cells.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between higher continuous `feature_073` and `objective_response` is more negative among patients with `feature_034` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_034` equal to 0.
The association between higher continuous `feature_053` and `objective_response` is more negative among patients with `feature_068` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_068` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_073:feature_034` had interaction coefficient -0.01281 per SD of `feature_073` among `feature_034` positive patients.
`feature_053:feature_068` had interaction coefficient -0.01987 per SD of `feature_053` among `feature_068` positive patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_018` equal to 1 has a lower `objective_response` rate than `feature_018` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_018` positive patients responded at 0.18569 versus 0.18782 when absent; risk difference -0.00214. This hypothesis was not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the final integrated model, the dominant positive subgroup pattern is the joint presence of `feature_015`, `feature_025`, and `feature_023`, while `feature_027`, `feature_004`, `feature_031`, `feature_033`, and `feature_053` remain negative main-effect predictors of `objective_response`.

analyses.result_summary: The final model retained a large `feature_015:feature_025:feature_023` coefficient (+0.46472). Negative main effects persisted for standardized `feature_027` (-0.03575), `feature_004` (-0.02035), `feature_031` (-0.01451), `feature_033` (-0.00796), and `feature_053` (-0.00674).
